model.label: Qwen 3.5 Flash
model.description: Alibaba • Suitable for simple tasks, fast and low cost.

pick.rationale: Arizona enters this Elite 8 matchup as one of the nation's elite programs ranked 3rd in both Torvik and KenPom systems with a staggering +37.56 efficiency margin compared to Gonzaga's +28.16—a nearly 10-point disparity that reflects superior balance across both ends of the floor. While Gonzaga boasts an elite defensive rating ranked 9th nationally at 93.9 adjD, Arizona's own defensive dominance at 89.7 adjD combined with their elite offensive production ranked 7th at 127.2 adjO creates a clear advantage. The Wildcats' significantly better record (31-2 vs 30-3) and their more dominant 78% confidence Sweet 16 victory over Arkansas versus Gonzaga's narrow 58% confidence win against Purdue demonstrates Arizona is playing at a higher tournament level.

reasoningStep.title: Advanced Metrics Comparison
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona holds commanding advantages across all key advanced metrics including rankings, efficiency margin, and balanced performance on both offense and defense.
reasoningStep.evidence: Arizona ranks 3rd in both Torvik/KenPom with +37.56 efficiency margin, 7th-ranked offense (127.2 adjO), and 3rd-ranked defense (89.7 adjD). Gonzaga ranks 11th in both systems with +28.16 efficiency margin, 30th-ranked offense (122.1 adjO), and 9th-ranked defense (93.9 adjD). The nearly 10-point efficiency margin gap is decisive.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

modelTrace.content:
Good, I have the CBB ratings for both teams. Let me analyze:

Arizona:
- Torvik Rank: 3rd
- KenPom Rank: 3rd
- Record: 31-2
- AdjO: 127.2 (7th nationally)
- AdjD: 89.7 (elite defense)
- AdjEM: +37.56 (staggering efficiency margin)

Gonzaga:
- Torvik Rank: 11th
- KenPom Rank: 11th
- Record: 30-3
- AdjO: 122.1 (30th nationally)
- AdjD: 93.9 (top 10 defense)
- AdjEM: +28.16 (strong but not elite)

The key differences:
1. Arizona is ranked significantly higher (3rd vs 11th in both systems)
2. Arizona has a much better efficiency margin (+37.56 vs +28.16 - nearly 10 point gap)
3. Arizona's offense is elite (7th vs 30th)
4. Both have strong defenses, but Arizona's is slightly better (89.7 vs 93.9)
5. Arizona has a better record (31-2 vs 30-3)

From the prior picks:
- Arizona won s16-west-1 against Arkansas with 78% confidence
- Gonzaga won s16-west-2 against Purdue with only 58% confidence

This suggests Arizona was more dominant in their Sweet 16 matchup than Gonzaga was.

The web search didn't reveal any specific injury concerns for either team in this matchup. The snippet mentions Texas Tech and North Carolina injuries but nothing about Arizona or Gonzaga specifically.

Based on the data:
- Arizona is clearly the superior team across all metrics
- The efficiency margin gap is nearly 10 points
- Arizona's offense is elite while Gonzaga's is merely good
- Both have strong defenses, but Arizona's edge there is also clear


- Arizona's tournament performance shows greater dominance

I'm confident Arizona will advance to the Final Four, leveraging their superior metrics and consistent high-level play throughout the tournament.
